betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6High-Speed Assembly: The Crafter§f

§aAutomate complex multi-step recipes at blazing speeds inside a single block!§f

Crafting items via traditional auto-crafters can be slow and require massive piping networks. The RFTools §bCrafter§f solves this by storing multiple recipes and handling internal steps instantly.

§6Crafter Tiers§f:
All crafters store up to §e50,000 RF§f. The tiers determine how many recipes can be configured inside one machine:
§b• Crafter Tier 1§f: Stores up to §e2 recipes§f.
§b• Crafter Tier 2§f: Stores up to §e4 recipes§f.
§b• Crafter Tier 3§f: Stores up to §e8 recipes§f.

§6Internal Routing & Crafting Modes§f:
In the Crafter GUI, each recipe's output can be set to one of two modes:
§b• External (Ext)§f: Outputs the crafted item into the output slot (accessible by pipes and hoppers).
§b• Internal (Int)§f: Keeps the crafted item inside the machine's buffer to be used as an ingredient for a subsequent recipe.
  §eExample§f: With a Crafter Tier 3, you can set sugarcane to craft into §bPaper (Int)§f, paper and leather to craft into §bBooks (Int)§f, and books with wood/iron to craft into §bBibliocraft Bookcases (Ext)§f — all without any items leaving the machine until the final product is complete!

§6High-Speed Performance§f:
RFTools Crafters execute recipes almost instantly and can process multiple crafting operations §aper tick§f if supplied with enough power and items. This makes them ideal for high-throughput automated factories.

§eTip§f: For complex assembly chains, plan all internal output dependencies (Int) before the final external recipe (Ext) — the Crafter will pre-build buffers in the order you specify.
